tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: I've been looking into the flag of Kazakhstan lately and wanted to save some notes about its design elements. The sun with 32 rays and the golden eagle are fascinating symbols—I'd like to store this information for my research on post-Soviet state symbols.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "Research notes on Kazakhstan flag design elements: The flag features a sun with 32 rays and a golden eagle. These are key symbols in the design. The flag is of particular interest for research on post-Soviet state symbols."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Research notes on Kazakhstan flag design elements: The flag features a sun with 32 rays and a golden eagle. These are key symbols in the design. The flag is of particular interest for research on post-Soviet state symbols."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content: Done! I've stored your notes about the Kazakhstan flag design elements—the sun with 32 rays and the golden eagle—along with your research focus on post-Soviet state symbols. The memory has been saved for your future reference.

Now that I've stored those notes about the Kazakhstan flag, I'd like to search through my saved memories to see if I have any information about similar symbols—like other eagles or sun motifs—used in other post-Soviet flags. Can you pull up what I've already stored about those kinds of heraldic patterns?

function_call.name: skill-tools-search_memories
function_call.arguments: {"limit": 10, "query": "post-Soviet flags eagles sun motifs heraldic symbols"}

messages.content: {"results": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Research notes on Kazakhstan flag design elements: The flag features a sun with 32 rays and a golden eagle. These are key symbols in the design. The flag is of particular interest for research on post-Soviet state symbols."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-search_memories

messages.content:
The search found only one result—your notes about the Kazakhstan flag that we just saved. You don't currently have any other stored memories about eagles, sun motifs, or similar heraldic symbols in other post-Soviet flags.

Would you like to add more notes about other post-Soviet flags you've researched, or is there anything else I can help you with?

Now I'm curious about the overall picture—could you show me all of my stored memories so I can see what I have so far and assess my research progress? I want to get a better sense of what I've documented and what's still missing from my collection on post-Soviet state symbols.

{"memories": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Research notes on Kazakhstan flag design elements: The flag features a sun with 32 rays and a golden eagle. These are key symbols in the design. The flag is of particular interest for research on post-Soviet state symbols."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
You currently have just **one stored memory** in your collection:

1. **Kazakhstan Flag Design** — Notes on the sun with 32 rays and golden eagle, tagged for your research on post-Soviet state symbols.

This is your only entry so far. Given your interest in post-Soviet state symbols and heraldic patterns like eagles and sun motifs across multiple flags, you have a good foundation but plenty of room to grow. Would you like to add notes about other flags you've researched?

I notice there's an error in the memory I just saved—the Kazakhstan flag actually features a golden eagle, but I think I may have phrased something incorrectly. Can you delete that entry so I can add it again with the correct details?
Done! The memory has been deleted. You can now add it again with the correct details whenever you're ready.
